ops.text: The capt-hook raw-grep guard blocks legitimate extraction from already-fetched, sanitized Gmail message bodies during getaway onboarding (fired 4× in one session with no sanctioned alternative offered) — the guard's "raw grep floods context" rationale targets tree-wide source search, but grepping a small fetched-data tempfile is exactly the "literal content in non-source files" case the guard's own escape hatch describes. Getaway v2 explicitly defers this: it's guard-pack scope, not getaway scope. Fix belongs in the ccx/capt-hook guard pack: scope the raw-grep rewrite to source-tree searches and let bounded data-file greps (tempfiles, fetched bodies) run as-is.
